rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TAMIKO MOORE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/12/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) 15 days' required notice must be re-done and include the language required by Local Rule 14.1 D. 2) Please explain the legal basis/legal authority for the court ordering termination of the estate when there are assets in the estate and creditor claims. It appears this petition should be ordered off calendar and a petition for approval of status report should have been filed instead. 3) Administrator hasn't filed an acceptance or rejection of all creditor claims. 4) Please file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the court instructing Chase Bank. 5) Please explain all efforts to collect estate assets from Chase from May 2022 to September 2022. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/26/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF GEORGINA LAXMI LOREK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/9/22. Examiner unavailable. Also, no proper I&A has been filed with assets appraised by the probate referee. From Probate Referee's Guide (p. 24): DISTRIBUTIONS FROM ESTATES AND TRUSTS -- What goes on Attachment 2 A complete description of the source of the distribution or inheritance (i.e. 30% share in the Estate of Groucho Marx, deceased, Los Angeles Superior Court Case # LAP 12345). The vested interest of the estate in the right to receive distribution from the probate estate or trust or other entity should be inventoried as a single item. -- What the Probate Referee needs to know 1. A complete description of the distribution or expectancy 2. An itemization of estate assets as of the valuation date, including supporting documents 3. If the distribution has occurred, provide a copy of the Order for Distribution and a list of distributed assets 4. Receipt for assets received (if available) 5. If the Order for Distribution or Receipt are not available, a listing of assets may be presented in a cover letter or on the attachment 6. If the interest is in a trust, copies of trust documents and a list of trust assets as of the valuation date, should be submitted.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLES BOBB
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/2022 to allow e-filed documents to be processed. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain the legal basis for how Vanessa Bobb has priority for letters. Vanessa has not received a nomination by the alleged brothers/sisters of Decedent and does not appear to have priority for letters. 2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] 3) Not all alleged heirs have waived bond. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/10/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVA SMALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/10/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.]
